message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageCms.py,Tests/test_imagecms.py
note: My feature: add error_threshold param to Image.quantize(). I only need to touch Image.py and its quantize test file. Let me know what your feature touches so we can keep edits disjoint within Image.py if needed.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My feature: add sort_palette parameter to quantize() for post-quantization palette sorting. I need to edit Image.py's quantize method and its tests. Please propose your files so we keep edits disjoint (e.g. separate functions/sections in Image.py, or ideally a different file entirely).

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: We both need quantize() in Image.py + its test file, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Let's use disjoint insertion points instead: I'll add my new 'error_threshold' param as the LAST parameter in quantize()'s signature (after your 'sort_palette'), with my MSE-check logic isolated in its own small block after libimagequant/median-cut color reduction runs, not touching your sort logic. In the docstring I'll append a separate ':param error_threshold:' entry after yours. In Tests/test_image_quantize.py I'll add new test functions named test_error_threshold_* only, appended at the end of the file, not touching your sort_palette tests. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Both our features target quantize() in the same two files, so a fully disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: we both edit Image.py/test_image_quantize.py but keep changes non-overlapping in code: (1) I'll add my new 'sort_palette' parameter as the LAST parameter in the quantize() signature (after any params you add), with a small dedicated block of logic at the end of the method body, and add a separate test class/functions (e.g. TestImageQuantizeSortPalette) at the END of the test file. (2) Please add your 'error_threshold' param before mine in the signature and keep your logic/tests in their own separate block. That way our diffs touch different lines and won't semantically conflict. Reply ACCEPT if that works, or tell me where you plan to insert your parameter/logic so I can place mine after it.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed. I'll add sort_palette before your error_threshold in the quantize() signature and docstring, with my logic in its own block and tests in a new TestImageQuantizeSortPalette-style section, appended before your test_error_threshold_* functions. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed: I'll add 'error_threshold' as a new parameter in quantize()'s signature placed BEFORE your 'sort_palette' param, with a dedicated MSE-check block in the method body (isolated from your sort logic), plus a docstring entry for it before yours. My tests go in new functions/class (e.g. test_error_threshold_*) appended near the end of Tests/test_image_quantize.py, distinct from your TestImageQuantizeSortPalette. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Implementation complete: added error_threshold param (placed before where you'll add sort_palette) plus a private _get_quantize_colors_for_threshold() helper right above quantize(), and appended test_error_threshold_* functions at the end of Tests/test_image_quantize.py. All 12 tests in the file pass locally. My changes only touch the docstring, the signature, one new helper method, and a 3-line guard before the existing 'im = self._new(self.im.quantize(...))' call, so there should be no overlap with your sort_palette block.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Implementation complete: added 'sort_palette' param (None default) to quantize(), applied via new module-level helper _sort_palette_colors() (uses existing remap_palette()) in both the normal quantization path and the palette= reference-image path. Supports 'brightness' and 'rgb' sort modes, raises ValueError otherwise. Added test_quantize_sort_palette (parametrized), test_quantize_sort_palette_with_reference_palette, and test_quantize_sort_palette_invalid to Tests/test_image_quantize.py, appended after test_transparent_colors_equal. All existing + new tests in the file pass (11 passed, 1 skipped for missing libimagequant). Note: I don't see your error_threshold changes in my copy of the file, so we may be operating on separate sandboxes without shared disk state — worth flagging in case the final merge is done elsewhere.
